title: Thermodynamic stability of twisted domains in AgCrSe$_{2}$ thin films grown on lattice-matched YSZ(111) substrate
authors: Haruto Sato, Kota Mihara, Kenshin Inamura, Yusuke Tajima, Kazutaka Kudo, Jobu Matsuno, Junichi Shiogai
abstract: Control of structural domains in epitaxial thin films of functional materials is a fundamental technique to utilize their intrinsic physical and chemical properties in solid-state devices. In this study, we report on suppression of twisted-domain formation in thin-film growth of polar magnetic semiconductor AgCrSe$_{2}$ using pulsed-laser deposition. In exploring concomitant optimized growth temperature and Ag/Cr composition ratio of supply, we find the critical growth temperature ($T\mathrm{_{sub}}$) for obtaining single 60$^{\circ}$ domain in c-axis oriented AgCrSe$_{2}$ thin film on a lattice-matched (111) plane of the yttria-stabilized zirconia substrate. At temperatures below and above the critical $T\mathrm{_{sub}}$, metastable 0$^{\circ}$ domain in addition to the 60$^{\circ}$ domain emerges, indicating delicate energy balance of thermodynamic stability for obtaining the single-domain structure. Surface structural analysis using time-of-flight low-energy atom scattering spectroscopy reveals the presence of two polar orientations along $+Z$ and $-Z$ directions. These findings provide valuable insights into the thin-film growth mechanisms for a family of two-dimensional compounds with rhombohedral lattices.
summary: This paper demonstrates how to suppress unwanted twisted domains in AgCrSe2 thin films by optimizing growth temperature and Ag/Cr ratios. By finding a critical temperature window, the authors achieved single-domain 60-degree oriented films, which is crucial for device applications.
main_problem: The study aims to control structural domains (twisted and polar) in epitaxial AgCrSe2 thin films to utilize their intrinsic physical properties.
main_result: The researchers identified a critical growth temperature for achieving a single 60-degree domain and found that Ag-rich targets help maintain stoichiometry at higher temperatures.
method: Pulsed-laser deposition (PLD) was used for growth, with structural characterization via XRD, AFM, EDX, and TOFLAS for polarity analysis.
model_or_system: The system consists of AgCrSe2, a polar magnetic semiconductor with a rhombohedral lattice, grown epitaxially on a lattice-matched YSZ(111) substrate.
key_observables: In-plane domain orientation (0 vs 60 degrees), polar orientation (+Z vs -Z), surface roughness (RMS), and Ag/Cr stoichiometry.
important_parameters: Substrate temperature (Tsub), Ag/Cr target composition ratio, and the Ag re-evaporation-driven decomposition regime.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Crystal structure and domain schematics; Fig 2: Dependence of roughness and stoichiometry on temperature with morphology images; Fig 3: In-plane XRD phi-scans and temperature dependence of domain fraction; Fig 4: TOFLAS pole figures and atomic arrangement.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the material and domain problem, details the growth and characterization methods, presents results on stoichiometry and surface quality, analyzes the temperature-dependent domain dynamics, and concludes with polarity analysis via TOFLAS.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
